payload.author: claude
payload.body:
Round 2 — MAJOR reframe (sharpens thesis, strengthens willingness-to-pay).

PRIMARY ACTOR IS AGENTS, NOT HUMANS. The killer use case: multiple LLM-driven coding agents working in parallel against one board must not COLLIDE. LLMs render code fast; two agents grabbing the same task = wasted tokens + rework = real dollar cost. Realtime sync exists to coordinate agents (and the humans steering them) so claims propagate instantly. This is a far stronger 'take my money' than human-human coordination because the cost of the gap is quantifiable.

DIFFERENTIATOR (re-reframed again): not merge correctness, not even human presence — it is that SCOPE IS THE BOARD AGENTS NATIVELY DRIVE. CLI + --json on every command + MCP server are ALREADY BUILT and are the unique agent-control surface vs Linear/Trello/Convex. The realtime relay makes that agent-driven board multiplayer.

PRODUCT SHAPE (bri's words): 'project' structure -> authenticate -> syncing starts. Humans collaborate through team-member accounts; agents act on the board via the CLI under those accounts.

BUSINESS MODEL: Scope-the-board-as-a-SaaS (agent-operated kanban), NOT a resold realtime/auth backend. The 'hosted realtime auth + todo streaming' framing is the MECHANISM, not the sold product.

OPEN FOR ROUND 3: concrete pricing/willingness; whether offline-first is still v1-critical (agents are usually online -> may let us ship online-realtime-relay first, defer offline); and account/agent identity model (do agents get own identity or act as a human's account?).
